worth_it_explanation: Comscore Cross-Platform Campaign Results measures deduplicated audience reach across open web, mobile, connected TV, and select walled-garden platforms. The measurement scope differs by environment: open web placements use census-level tag measurement, mobile apps require the publisher to have integrated a compatible SDK, CTV placements have their own tag and integration requirements, and walled-garden platforms like YouTube and Meta operate through platform-specific Comscore integrations rather than direct tag access. These checks confirm that the practitioner understands which environments in a given campaign are measured by CCR, how walled-garden measurement works and where its scope differs from open-web tag measurement, and whether any platform receives no CCR coverage, which affects how much of the cross-platform picture is complete.
intro: Comscore Cross-Platform Campaign Results (CCR), formerly Comscore Campaign Ratings, measures deduplicated unique reach, frequency, gross rating points (GRPs), and on-target percentage for a campaign across desktop, mobile, CTV, and select social and audio platforms. Coverage is not uniform across environments. Open web placements receive census-level measurement through Comscore tags served with the ad. Mobile app placements require the app publisher to have integrated a compatible third-party measurement SDK, such as the IAB Tech Lab Open Measurement SDK (OM SDK), and to have activated Comscore as a measurement partner. CTV measurement depends on the device operating system and the integration path used. Walled-garden platforms like YouTube and Meta provide measurement through platform-specific Comscore integrations that differ in scope from open-web direct-tag measurement. These checks confirm that a practitioner reviewing a CCR report understands which platforms and environments are fully measured, which are partially measured, and which are outside CCR's coverage for a given campaign.

audit_checks.title: CTV and mobile app environments are assumed to be measured by CCR without confirming SDK or integration requirements are met
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When a practitioner reads a CCR cross-platform report without confirming that CTV and mobile app placements were actually covered, the reported reach figure may reflect only the open web portion of the campaign. A campaign that allocates substantial budget to CTV or in-app inventory but has incomplete CCR coverage on those environments will show a lower total reach than the campaign actually produced, and the cross-platform deduplication will be incomplete.

educate.base:
Comscore Cross-Platform Campaign Results (CCR) can measure audience reach across desktop, mobile web, mobile app, and connected TV (CTV) environments, but measurement in each environment depends on how Comscore receives impression-level signals from that environment.

Open web desktop and mobile web placements use a census-level tag approach: a Comscore measurement tag travels with the ad creative and fires when the ad is served. This approach gives CCR direct, impression-level coverage of open web inventory without requiring any action from the publisher beyond allowing the tag to fire.

Mobile app environments work differently. A mobile app runs inside a sandboxed container, and a measurement tag embedded in an ad creative cannot directly access the device signals it would need to report to Comscore. Coverage in mobile apps depends on the app publisher integrating a third-party measurement Software Development Kit (SDK). The IAB Tech Lab's Open Measurement SDK (OM SDK) is the industry standard for this purpose. An app that has integrated the OM SDK and activated Comscore as a measurement partner can pass impression signals to CCR. An app that has not integrated the OM SDK, or that has not enabled Comscore specifically, cannot be measured by CCR the same way an open web placement can. Not every app in every buying platform supports CCR measurement, and the coverage varies by publisher.

CTV measurement depends on the device and the integration path. CTV placements delivered through open web-connected inventory may receive tag-based measurement. Placements delivered through CTV-native environments, such as smart TV operating systems or streaming app publishers, require the publisher to have integrated an appropriate Comscore measurement solution for that environment. The IAB Tech Lab's OM SDK extended its coverage to CTV in 2022, and Comscore has expanded its CTV integrations, but coverage is not universal across all CTV publishers and inventory types.

A few signs this check applies to a campaign:
  • The CCR setup was completed based on open web tag deployment only, without confirming whether mobile app publishers and CTV publishers in the plan support Comscore measurement
  • Mobile app or CTV inventory is a significant share of the campaign budget, but the CCR report does not show a breakdown of impressions and reach by environment, making it unclear whether those environments contributed to the measured total
  • The CCR impression count is notably lower than the buying platform's reported impression total, with no documented explanation for which environments are excluded from CCR's measurement scope for this campaign
You may want to confirm which environments in the campaign's media plan are covered by CCR before reading the cross-platform reach figure as a complete picture of who the campaign reached.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an ecommerce advertiser running a campaign across display, in-app, and CTV inventory, a CCR report that does not confirm mobile app and CTV coverage may be reporting only the open web portion of the reach. You may want to confirm which inventory types in the plan were covered by Comscore measurement before using the reported reach figure for audience planning or post-campaign analysis.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser using a significant share of in-app or CTV inventory to build awareness, incomplete CCR coverage on those environments means the audience built through those channels is not reflected in the cross-platform reach or frequency figures. You may want to confirm coverage before the flight launches so the CCR output reflects the full audience the campaign is designed to reach.

learn_more.source_title: Open Measurement SDK: enabling third-party measurement in mobile apps and CTV

educate.learn_more_note: Comscore documentation covering SDK and integration requirements for mobile app and CTV measurement is accessible to credentialed account holders through the CCR portal. Contact your Comscore account representative for guidance on confirming which publishers in your plan support Comscore measurement and how to identify coverage gaps before a campaign launches.

impactful_updates.update_title: Comscore Campaign Ratings renamed Cross-Platform Campaign Results (December 2025)
impactful_updates.update_summary: Comscore renamed Campaign Ratings to Cross-Platform Campaign Results (CCR) in December 2025, expanding measurement coverage to include streaming audio and social alongside the existing digital, CTV, and TV measurement. The reach, frequency, GRP, and on-target percentage metrics and the CCR acronym carry forward under the new name.

investigate.intro: Confirm which environments in the campaign are covered by CCR measurement, whether mobile app publishers in the plan have integrated a compatible SDK and enabled Comscore, and whether the CCR impression total is consistent with the buying platform's total for those environments.

comscore.label: Comscore
steps: Log in to the Comscore CCR portal and open the campaign in question. If the portal provides a breakdown of impressions by environment (desktop, mobile web, mobile app, CTV), review that breakdown and note which environments contributed to the measured total.

Compare the total impressions Comscore reports against the buying platform's impression total for the same flight dates. A substantial gap between the two figures, without a documented explanation, may indicate that one or more environments are not covered by CCR.

Review the campaign's media plan to identify which publishers and inventory types received significant budget. For each mobile app or CTV publisher, confirm with your Comscore account representative or the publisher's ad operations team whether that publisher has integrated Comscore measurement for that environment.

If the plan uses a demand-side platform (DSP) to buy in-app or CTV inventory, ask the DSP or your Comscore account team whether Comscore measurement tags are passed through programmatic in-app and CTV buys on that DSP.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records which environments in the campaign are confirmed as covered by CCR measurement. A campaign where mobile app and CTV coverage is confirmed through publisher or DSP verification, or where those environments represent a negligible share of total impressions, passes. A campaign where mobile app or CTV is a meaningful share of budget and coverage on those environments has not been confirmed is a fail, recorded with the environment, the estimated impression share for that environment, and the CCR-to-buying-platform impression discrepancy if available.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record which environments are confirmed as covered by CCR and the buying-platform impression total versus the CCR impression total above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that the Comscore Cross-Platform Campaign Results (CCR) setup for {{site_name}}'s campaign has not confirmed measurement coverage across all environments in the media plan. CCR open web coverage relies on tag-based measurement, but mobile app coverage requires the publisher to have integrated a compatible SDK and enabled Comscore, and CTV coverage depends on the integration path used for each publisher. Without confirming coverage before the flight, the reported reach figure may reflect only a portion of the campaign's actual audience. {{voice_recommend}} verifying with your Comscore account representative which publishers in the plan support CCR measurement in mobile app and CTV environments before the campaign launches, and documenting any environments excluded from CCR coverage so the reported cross-platform reach figure is interpreted with the correct scope.

audit_checks.title: Walled-garden platforms are compared against open-web inventory in CCR without accounting for differences in measurement scope and methodology
audit_checks.impact_rationale: YouTube and Meta measurement in CCR operates through platform-specific integrations that differ in scope from the direct-tag census measurement used for open web inventory. When practitioners compare on-target percentage, reach, or frequency across walled-garden and open-web line items without understanding these differences, they may draw performance conclusions from a comparison that does not hold the measurement methodology constant.

educate.base:
Comscore Cross-Platform Campaign Results (CCR) measures walled-garden platforms through integrations that differ from the tag-based census measurement used for open web inventory. On the open web, a Comscore tag travels with the ad creative, fires at impression time, and provides direct, census-level measurement of each impression. Walled-garden platforms do not allow a third-party tag to fire independently on every impression the way open web inventory does.

YouTube measurement in CCR operates through an integration between Comscore and YouTube using Google's Ads Data Hub and related APIs. This integration enables CCR to report reach and frequency for YouTube campaign impressions alongside open web impressions in the same cross-platform report, including deduplication across the two environments. However, the measurement relies on data that YouTube makes available through that integration, and the data model differs from direct-tag census measurement. Comscore integrated Campaign Ratings with YouTube in 2024, covering YouTube Shorts, In-Feed inventory, standard video, YouTube TV, and Masthead ads across CTV, desktop, and mobile.

Meta measurement in CCR operates through a comparable integration covering Facebook and Instagram campaign data. As of December 2025, Comscore expanded CCR to include social reporting from Meta platforms, enabling deduplicated reach across Meta and other environments in the same CCR report. As with YouTube, this integration reflects data that Meta makes available to Comscore through the integration, and the scope of what can be measured within Meta's environment is determined by that integration rather than by a Comscore-controlled tag.

The practical implication for practitioners reading a CCR report is that on-target percentage, viewability, and impression-level data from walled-garden platforms may not be available in the same form or at the same granularity as open web data. Comparing on-target percentage between a YouTube line item and an open web display line item without accounting for this difference can lead to conclusions about relative performance that reflect measurement scope more than actual delivery quality.

A few signs this check applies:
  • A CCR post-campaign report compares on-target percentage or frequency across YouTube, Meta, and open web line items without any note about the different measurement methodology for each environment
  • The team concludes that open web inventory outperformed YouTube or Meta based on CCR metrics without confirming that the compared metrics are measured consistently across those environments
  • The CCR report includes walled-garden impression and reach data but no documentation was reviewed at campaign setup confirming what is and is not available through the platform integration for those environments
You may want to confirm with your Comscore account representative what data each walled-garden integration provides and what its scope limitations are before drawing cross-environment performance comparisons from a CCR report.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an ecommerce advertiser comparing CCR performance across a YouTube awareness campaign and open web display, the on-target percentage and frequency data for YouTube come through Comscore's YouTube integration rather than direct tag measurement. You may want to confirm what the integration covers and what it excludes before using those figures to make budget allocation decisions between YouTube and open web inventory.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser running awareness across Meta, YouTube, and open web display in a single CCR measurement period, the cross-platform deduplication is a key output, but the scope of what Comscore can measure within each walled garden differs from what it can measure on the open web. You may want to confirm those scope differences with your Comscore account team before interpreting the per-platform reach figures as directly comparable.

educate.learn_more_note: Comscore documentation on the scope and methodology of its walled-garden platform integrations, including what data is available through the YouTube and Meta integrations and what limitations apply, is accessible to credentialed account holders through the CCR portal. Contact your Comscore account representative for a full accounting of what each integration measures and how the data compares to open web tag measurement.

impactful_updates.update_title: Comscore expands CCR social measurement to include Meta platforms (December 2025)
impactful_updates.update_summary: In December 2025, Comscore expanded Cross-Platform Campaign Results to include social reporting from Meta platforms (Facebook and Instagram), enabling deduplicated reach across Meta and other environments in the same CCR report. The Meta measurement operates through a platform integration, as with YouTube, rather than through direct Comscore tag deployment within Meta's environment.

impactful_updates.update_title: Comscore Campaign Ratings integrated with YouTube (2024)
impactful_updates.update_summary: Comscore extended Campaign Ratings to measure YouTube audiences through a platform-specific integration, distinct from open-web tag measurement.

investigate.intro: Review whether the CCR report includes walled-garden platform data and, if so, confirm what the platform integration covers and what limitations apply before drawing cross-environment performance comparisons.

comscore.label: Comscore
steps: Log in to the Comscore CCR portal and open the campaign report. Identify whether the report includes YouTube, Meta, or other walled-garden environments alongside open web inventory.

If walled-garden environments are present, review the report's methodology notes or ask your Comscore account representative to confirm which metrics for those environments come through platform integrations rather than direct tag measurement.

Confirm whether on-target percentage is reported for the walled-garden environments in this campaign, and if so, confirm whether the methodology for that metric is comparable to the open web on-target percentage in the same report.

Review any cross-environment comparison in the post-campaign analysis and note whether the comparison accounts for measurement scope differences, or whether open web and walled-garden metrics are presented as directly equivalent without qualification.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether the campaign's CCR report includes walled-garden platform data, whether the methodology for walled-garden metrics was confirmed before the report was used for performance conclusions, and whether any cross-environment comparisons note the measurement scope difference. A report where walled-garden measurement scope was confirmed at setup and cross-environment comparisons are qualified accordingly passes. A report where walled-garden and open web metrics are compared directly with no documentation of the methodology difference is a fail, recorded with the specific comparison made and the metric used.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record whether walled-garden measurement scope was confirmed and whether cross-environment comparisons in the CCR report are qualified for methodology differences above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s CCR reports compare metrics across walled-garden platforms (YouTube and Meta) and open web inventory without documenting the differences in measurement methodology between those environments. Walled-garden measurement in CCR operates through platform-specific integrations rather than the direct-tag census measurement used on the open web, which means the scope of available metrics and the data model differ by environment. Treating these as equivalent in a cross-environment performance comparison can lead to conclusions that reflect measurement scope rather than actual delivery quality. {{voice_recommend}} confirming with your Comscore account representative what each walled-garden integration measures and where its scope differs from open web tag measurement, and qualifying any cross-environment comparisons in CCR reports with those scope differences noted.

audit_checks.title: A platform with no or partial Comscore coverage is included in the cross-platform plan without flagging the measurement gap
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When a platform that receives little or no CCR coverage is included in a cross-platform plan alongside measured inventory, the audience reached through that platform does not appear in the CCR report. The campaign's reported reach is understated, the frequency distribution is incomplete, and any performance conclusion drawn from the CCR data does not account for the portion of the audience reached through the unmeasured platform.

educate.base:
Not every platform in a multi-channel media plan can be measured by Comscore Cross-Platform Campaign Results (CCR) to the same degree. Measurement requires either a Comscore tag that travels with the ad creative, a publisher-level SDK integration, or a platform-specific Comscore integration. When a platform in the plan does not support any of these paths for a given campaign, that platform's impressions and the audience reached through it fall outside CCR's measurement scope for that campaign.

This situation is different from incomplete measurement due to a missed setup step, where the fix is to complete the setup. It covers cases where CCR measurement is not available for a platform because the platform does not support the required integration, the publisher has not activated Comscore measurement, or the platform operates in a way that prevents standard Comscore tag or SDK measurement. In these cases, the gap is a known constraint, not an error to fix, but it needs to be documented so the CCR report is read with the correct scope in mind.

Common scenarios where partial or no CCR coverage may apply include audio-only ad placements (where no visual impression fires a measurement tag), some smaller or niche app publishers that have not integrated an OM SDK-compatible measurement solution, programmatic inventory delivered through exchanges that do not pass Comscore tags to certain environments, and platforms in international markets where Comscore's local measurement footprint may not yet cover that environment.

The issue arises not when coverage is incomplete, but when that incompleteness is not accounted for. A campaign that allocates 20% of its budget to a platform with no CCR coverage and then draws conclusions about total cross-platform reach from the CCR report will arrive at a reach figure that represents at most 80% of the actual audience reached, with no indication in the report that a portion of the campaign is absent.

A few signs this check applies:
  • The media plan includes platforms or publishers that were not part of the CCR setup discussion, and no one has confirmed whether those platforms are covered by CCR for this campaign
  • The CCR impression total is notably lower than the sum of the buying platform impression totals across all line items, with no documented explanation for which platforms account for the discrepancy
  • A platform that contributes a meaningful share of campaign impressions appears in the buying platform report but not in the CCR environment breakdown
You may want to identify any platforms or publishers in the plan that are not confirmed as CCR-covered and document those gaps before the campaign launches, so the CCR report is interpreted with a clear account of what is and is not included.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an ecommerce advertiser running a campaign that includes a mix of programmatic display, in-app inventory, CTV, and podcast audio, the audio placements are likely outside CCR's measurement scope because audio ads do not fire a visual impression tag. You may want to confirm before the campaign launches which environments are confirmed as CCR-covered and note the audio budget share as an unmeasured portion of the cross-platform plan.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser using a mix of established and newer or niche publishers to reach a specific professional audience, not all publishers may have Comscore measurement activated for their inventory. You may want to confirm CCR coverage on each publisher in the plan and note any publishers outside that scope when presenting cross-platform reach figures to stakeholders.

educate.learn_more_note: Comscore documentation covering which platforms and publisher types are supported for CCR measurement, and how to identify coverage gaps in a media plan before a campaign launches, is accessible to credentialed account holders through the CCR portal. Contact your Comscore account representative for a pre-campaign coverage assessment of the platforms included in a specific media plan.

investigate.intro: Identify all platforms and publishers included in the media plan, confirm which are covered by CCR, and document any that are not so the reported cross-platform reach can be interpreted with the correct scope.

comscore.label: Comscore
steps: List all platforms and publishers included in the campaign's media plan that received budget. For each, note whether it was part of the CCR setup discussion or confirmed as a covered environment.

Compare the buying platform's impression total for the flight against the CCR impression total for the same period. Calculate the difference as a percentage of the total buying-platform impressions to estimate what share of campaign impressions are outside CCR's scope.

For any platform with a meaningful impression share that does not appear in the CCR environment breakdown, confirm with your Comscore account representative whether that platform supports CCR measurement and, if not, why.
Document the confirmed unmeasured platforms and their estimated impression share so that anyone reviewing the CCR report understands the scope of the reported reach figure.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether any platforms or publishers in the media plan are outside CCR's measurement scope, the estimated impression share those platforms represent, and whether that gap was documented before the CCR report was used for performance conclusions. A campaign where unmeasured platforms represent a negligible impression share, or where gaps are documented and accounted for in the report interpretation, passes. A campaign where a platform with a meaningful impression share is outside CCR's scope and that gap is undocumented is a fail, recorded with the platform name and the estimated impression share outside CCR coverage.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record which platforms in the plan are outside CCR coverage and their estimated impression share above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s cross-platform media plan includes one or more platforms that are outside Comscore Cross-Platform Campaign Results (CCR) measurement scope, and that gap has not been documented in the campaign's CCR reporting. The audience reached through those platforms does not appear in the CCR report, which means the reported cross-platform reach understates the total audience the campaign reached, and the frequency distribution and on-target percentage calculations are based on an incomplete picture of the campaign's delivery. {{voice_recommend}} conducting a pre-campaign CCR coverage review with your Comscore account representative for any media plan that includes multiple environments, documenting any platforms that fall outside CCR's scope and their estimated impression share, and noting that scope clearly when presenting CCR cross-platform reach figures to stakeholders or using them for planning.
